title: PM Durable DB-Backed Idempotency Store
objective: Implement the smallest bounded follow-on to packet 019 by replacing the process-local PM POST idempotency cache with a durable database-backed store for the existing seven PM POST handlers so replay and mismatch semantics survive process restarts and coordinate across multi-instance deployments, while preserving the current `Idempotency-Key` header contract, keeping the work schema registry at 22, keeping `/api/v1/work/*` path count at 15, and avoiding any broader PM mutation expansion or `work.wbs_nodes` writes.
constrained_outputs: One bounded shared-infra durable idempotency store for the existing PM POST handlers only, including the minimum persisted key, route, body-hash, response, and expiry fields needed to preserve packet-019 replay and mismatch semantics across restarts and multi-instance deployments

Runtime wiring that replaces the process-local PM idempotency cache with the durable store for `projects`, `work_packages`, `tasks`, `assignments`, `dependencies`, `execution_issues`, and `progress_snapshots` without changing endpoint inventory, registry size, or path counts

Focused regression evidence proving matching replays still return cached responses byte-for-byte, payload mismatches still return a merged-error-shaped 422, validation-failure 422s are still not persisted, and the seven PATCH handlers remain untouched in behavior
A bounded handoff naming residual cleanup or expiry work without broadening PM mutation scope or opening `work.wbs_nodes` writes
validation_steps: Confirm packet 019f touches only the bounded durable-idempotency seam for the seven existing PM POST handlers and does not add WBS-node writes, new endpoints, or broader PM entity mutation scope

Confirm the durable store preserves the packet-019 `Idempotency-Key` header contract, route scoping, canonical-body hashing, byte-for-byte replay behavior, mismatch 422 shape, and the rule that validation-failure 422s are not persisted

Confirm the durable store is backed by bounded shared-infra SQL and coordinates across process restarts and multi-instance deployments without increasing the work schema registry beyond 22 or `/api/v1/work/*` path count beyond 15

Confirm packet-010a, packet-010b, packet-011f, packet-013, packet-013i, packet-013j, packet-014, packet-014i, packet-015, packet-015i, packet-016, packet-017, packet-018, and packet-019 surfaces still pass or skip as expected after the durable-store adoption
sql_permission_note: Packet 019f may author only the minimum shared-infra SQL needed for a durable PM idempotency store backing the seven existing POST handlers. No unrelated schema edits, new PM entities, new endpoints, or WBS-node writes are authorized.
